plan.action: Verify lib/branch.sh has branch_name (deterministic slug generation: lowercase, hyphenate spaces/underscores, strip non-alphanumeric, collapse hyphens, trim) producing 'feature/<id>-<slug>' format
Verify lib/branch.sh has branch_ensure that checks show-ref for existing branch (reuse with checkout) or creates new from base (checkout -b), returning 1 with ERROR message on any git failure
Verify lib/loop.sh loop_run_iteration calls branch_name then branch_ensure and returns 1 with clear error if branch_ensure fails (stopping the iteration)

Verify tests/branch.bats covers: format output, lowercase slug, spaces-to-hyphens, strip specials, collapse hyphens, determinism, missing-arg failures, branch creation, branch reuse, failure on bad base
Run shellcheck on lib/branch.sh and lib/loop.sh to confirm typecheck passes with no errors
Run bats tests/branch.bats to confirm all tests pass

testing_recommendations: branch_name with same inputs twice must produce identical output (determinism)
branch_name with UPPER CASE title produces all-lowercase slug
branch_name with special chars (colons, parens, slashes) strips them cleanly
branch_name with multiple consecutive spaces collapses to single hyphens and trims leading/trailing
branch_name with no args returns non-zero exit
branch_ensure on a fresh repo creates the branch and switches HEAD to it
branch_ensure when branch already exists checks it out, prints 'Reusing existing branch', returns 0
branch_ensure with an invalid base branch returns 1 and prints ERROR to stderr
loop_run_iteration returns 1 with ERROR output when branch_ensure fails (stub git to fail on checkout -b)
risks: lib/branch.sh has unstaged changes (per git status) — verify the working-tree version is complete and consistent with tests before running
bash 3.2 (macOS default): sed -E portability — ensure extended regex flag works; prefer [[:upper:]] over \u
branch_ensure's untracked-file conflict recovery uses awk to parse git error output — fragile if git message format changes across versions
